records.ety: [L. effectus, fr. efficere, effectum, to effect; ex + facere to make: cf. F. effet, formerly also spelled effect. See Fact.]
usage: These words indicate things which arise out of some antecedent, or follow as a consequent. Effect, which may be regarded as the generic term, denotes that which springs directly from something which can properly be termed a cause. A consequence is more remote, not being strictly caused, nor yet a mere sequence, but following out of and following indirectly, or in the train of events, something on which it truly depends. A result is still more remote and variable, like the rebound of an elastic body which falls in very different directions. We may foresee the effects of a measure, may conjecture its consequences, but can rarely discover its final results.

defs: Execution; performance; realization; operation; as, the law goes into effect in May.
quotes: That no compunctious visitings of nature Shake my fell purpose, nor keep peace between The effect and it.
All the large effects That troop with majesty.

defs: In general: That which is produced by an agent or cause; the event which follows immediately from an antecedent, called the cause; result; consequence; outcome; fruit; as, the effect of luxury.
quotes: The effect is the unfailing index of the amount of the cause.

defs: Impression left on the mind; sensation produced.
quotes: The effect was heightened by the wild and lonely nature of the place.

defs: Power to produce results; efficiency; force; importance; account; as, to speak with effect.
Consequence intended; purpose; meaning; general intent; -- with to.
quotes: They spake to her to that effect.

defs: The purport; the sum and substance.
Reality; actual meaning; fact, as distinguished from mere appearance.
quotes: No other in effect than what it seems.

defs: Goods; movables; personal estate; -- sometimes used to embrace real as well as personal property; as, the people escaped from the town with their effects.
quotes: Resolving all events, with their effects And manifold results, into the will And arbitration wise of the Supreme.
Shun the bitter consequence, for know, The day thou eatest thereof, . . . thou shalt die.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Effected; p. pr. & vb. n. Effecting.]

defs: To produce, as a cause or agent; to cause to be.
quotes: So great a body such exploits to effect.

defs: To bring to pass; to execute; to enforce; to achieve; to accomplish.
quotes: To effect that which the divine counsels had decreed.
They sailed away without effecting their purpose.
